Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Complex The FunnyDream campaign, with basic skill levels, targets the Energy sector with a multi-stage complex cyber attack. The attackers engage in extensive reconnaissance, resource development, and employ sophisticated techniques to gain initial access, maintain persistence, escalate privileges, evade defenses, access credentials, discover network details, move laterally, collect sensitive data, exfiltrate information, and finally impact the system by inhibiting recovery efforts.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Scheduled Task, Public IP/Network Block, Domain Name Registrar, File, Target System Artifact, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Response Metadata (Mechanism), Command Execution (Mechanism), Scheduled Task/Job, Application Log Content (Mechanism), User Account Authentication (Mechanism), Network Service Scanning, Remote Desktop Protocol, File Access (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, Process Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers initiate their operation by conducting active scanning to map out the network topology of the energy sector target, identifying potential entry points and vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: To support their operations, the attackers acquire infrastructure such as fake domains and servers to host phishing pages and malware.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Using the acquired infrastructure, attackers send phishing emails with malicious attachments or links to trick unsuspecting employees into granting initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 663 Name: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: Once inside, the attackers trick users into executing malicious files, leading to further compromise.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 654 Name: Credential Prompt Impersonation
stage.description: To maintain access, attackers create scheduled tasks that execute malware periodically.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121 Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: Attackers find and exploit vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to escalate their privileges within the system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 578 Name: Disable Security Software
stage.description: They impair defensive capabilities by disabling antivirus software to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 49 Name: Password Brute Forcing
stage.description: Attempting to gain credentials through repeated login attempts, the attackers brute-force weak accounts.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: After gaining access, attackers identify network services and vulnerabilities to plan lateral movement.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 220 Name: Client-Server Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: Utilizing credentials obtained, attackers move laterally across the network via RDP.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 328 Name: TCP 'RST' Flag Checksum Probe
stage.description: Attackers extract sensitive data from compromised machines to gather valuable information.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 Name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Stolen data is transferred back to attacker-controlled infrastructure using command and control channels.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Finally, attackers destroy backups or render them inaccessible, ensuring no recovery is possible.

Scenario.summary: The FunnyDream campaign represents a sophisticated yet basic-skilled threat actor targeting the energy sector with a multi-faceted approach. From initial reconnaissance to final impact, the attackers demonstrate a systematic methodology, employing various techniques to infiltrate, persist, and ultimately disrupt critical systems while evading detection.
